content:
Post 1
ASX 200 closed June at 8,823.40, up 0.68% on the final session. The index sits 4.12% below its 52-week high, with five-day performance flat. A cross above the 200-day moving average marks a key technical signal. #ASX #ausbiz #investing

Post 2
Consumer Staples led June at +13.90%, followed by Health Care +13.58% and Consumer Discretionary +12.50%. Energy was the worst performer at -9.23%, with Materials dropping -5.21%. A clear defensive and consumer rotation defined the month. #ASX #energy #materials #ausbiz

Post 3
Neuren Pharmaceuticals surged 36.07% in June, the top ASX 200 performer. Life360 added 11.64%. Neuren's move reflects strong biotech momentum driving outsized gains in the Health Care sector, up 13.58% for the month. #ASX #healthcare #biotech #ausbiz

Post 4
Energy fell 9.23% and Materials lost 5.21%, dragging on the index. Commodity weakness was the dominant macro headwind in June. Consumer sectors absorbed the rotation, with Staples gaining 13.90% as defensives outperformed. #ASX #commodities #energy #ironore #ausbiz

Post 5
Key risk: Energy at -9.23% monthly signals sustained commodity weakness that could pressure Materials further. Opportunity: ASX 200 at 8,823.40 just crossed its 200-day moving average, a level technicals traders will watch closely in July. #ASX #ausbiz #investing #trading
